ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific plum-flavoured green tea entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers ready-to-drink green tea; no plum-flavoured variant code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for plum-flavoured green tea beverage; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of plum green tea (approximately 250 ml / 250 g) as typically served at a Singaporean bubble tea or speciality tea stall without any topping.

nutrient_source.title: Plum Green Tea (No Topping) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for Plum Green Tea (No Topping), CR ID D08010094. All per-100 g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for plum-flavoured green tea with sugar. GI estimated at 55 (low; GI ≤55 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database convention) by analogy with sugar-sweetened tea beverages reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 18.725) / 100 ≈ 10.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (7.49 g/100 g) in a beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention, despite very low sodium (1 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request zero-sugar preparation; the base drink's 7.49 g/100 g sugar exceeds the 5 g/100 g beverage sugar threshold associated with independent blood pressure elevation (AHA 2018), and removing toppings alone does not address this risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre, producing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; liquid sugars are absorbed faster than solid food equivalents.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this drink in its standard form and request zero-sugar preparation, as the 7.49 g/100 g sugar with no fibre buffer causes rapid glycaemic excursions; a 250 ml serving delivers approximately 18.7 g of rapidly absorbed sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages at this sugar concentration (7.49 g/100 g) is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased LDL-C, and heightened cardiovascular risk independent of caloric intake.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage in its sweetened form, as habitual sugar-sweetened beverage intake is independently associated with increased cardiovascular mortality (Malik et al., Circulation 2010); zero-sugar ordering is recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 7.49 g/100 g with zero dietary fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; the green tea polyphenol content in this commercial sweetened drink is insufficient to provide meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should request zero-sugar preparation to potentially benefit from green tea catechins; in its sweetened form, the pro-inflammatory effect of added sugar outweighs any anti-inflammatory benefit from the tea base.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plum Green Tea (No Topping) is a commercially prepared, sugar-sweetened speciality tea beverage popular in Singapore, commonly sold at bubble tea and speciality tea stalls. It is made from freshly brewed green tea infused with plum flavouring and sweetened with sugar, served without any customisable toppings such as tapioca pearls or jelly. The drink has a light, slightly tart-sweet flavour profile with the characteristic grassy, vegetal notes of green tea balanced by the fruity, mildly sour character of plum. The texture is thin and liquid with no added solids. Nutritionally, it provides 29 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient at 7.49 g/100 g (all from sugar), negligible protein (0.01 g/100 g), and zero fat. Dietary fibre is absent and sodium is very low at 1 mg/100 g. A standard 250 ml serving delivers approximately 18.7 g of rapidly absorbed sugar, classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). Despite the green tea base containing catechins and polyphenols with potential antioxidant properties, the added sugar content negates meaningful health benefit in its standard form. Clinically, this drink is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease unless ordered with zero sugar. The removal of toppings reduces additional carbohydrate load but does not address the sugar content of the base drink. This record is sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010094).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010094) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical provenance. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010094); this is a sugar-sweetened tea beverage with no fat-containing ingredients. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this commercial plum green tea beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010094) as of 2026. GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008; GI 55 is classified as LOW per the University of Sydney three-tier scale (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70); gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' accordingly. No direct GI measurement for plum green tea exists in approved sources. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 250 g standard serving: GL = (55 × 18.725) / 100 ≈ 10.3.
